stimulus: The World Health Organization (WHO) publishes annual figures on the average cost of a standardized clinic visit across its member states. For example, in March 2022, a routine outpatient consultation priced at the equivalent of 50 US dollars (USD) in Germany would have cost 18 USD and 73 USD in fellow WHO ______ and Sweden, respectively.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice A is correct. No punctuation is needed between the title "fellow WHO members" and the coordinated proper nouns "Vietnam and Sweden," because the country names are essential to identifying which members are meant.

Choice B is incorrect because placing a comma after "members" treats the names as nonessential, suggesting they could be removed without changing the sentence’s meaning — but here the names are necessary. Choice C is incorrect because a semicolon cannot be used in this way; it would also wrongly imply two independent clauses. Choice D is incorrect because a colon after "members" would suggest that all WHO members had those costs, rather than just Vietnam and Sweden.
